CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100041908) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SILVA, CHRISTOPHER Z. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 3.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CINDI PARDINI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER SILVA. DEFENDANT PARDINI'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS' LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR SLANDER AND/OR LIBEL THAT (1) OCCURRED WITHIN A YEAR OF THE FILING OF THE INSTANT COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 28, 2018 (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 340(C)); AND (2) DOES NOT INVOLVE PRIVILEGED CONDUCT SUCH AS A POLICE REPORT OR STATEMENTS MADE IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. (SEE CIV. CODE 47; HAGBERG V. CALIFORNIA FEDERAL BANK (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 39.) PLAINTIFF SHALL REMOVE THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING TIME-BARRED OR PRIVILEGED CONDUCT. ORDER IS SIGNED.JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ADRIAN G. DRISCOLL, ESQ. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) appearing for defendant Cindi Ann Pardini;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CINDI ANN PARDINI'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Cindi Ann Pardini's demurrer to the second amended complaint of Plaintiff Christopher Silva is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not cured any of the defects in his prior complaints identified in the Court's orders sustaining demurrers to those complaints with leave to amend. The claims for defamation alleged in Paragraphs 12, 13, 17, and 18 of the SAC are time-barred. (Code Civ. Proc. 340(c).) Those claims also fail to allege with specificity the words which constituted the alleged defamation, as do the undated (and unauthorized) allegations of slander in paragraphs 15 and 16. The claim for alleged slander in Paragraph 14 of the SAC is barred by the absolute privilege of Civil Code section 47(b). Plaintiff's attempt, without leave of Court, to add a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails to state a claim for an independent tort. (See Felton v. Schaeffer (1991) 229 Cal.App.3d 229, 239 [courts have rejected attempts to avoid the strictures and defenses of libel law merely by artful pleading]; Flynn v. Higham (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 677, 681-682 ["to allow an independent cause of action for the intentional infliction of emotional distress, based on the same acts which would not support a defamation action, would allow plaintiffs to do indirectly that which they could not do directly. It would also render meaningless any defense of truth or privilege."].)

In his "answer" to the demurrer, Plaintiff fails to proffer any intelligible allegations that could overcome the defects in the SAC, and indeed asserts that he intends to file a new lawsuit in Kansas, where he now apparently resides. Accordingly, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order and separate judgment both signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christoper Silva, 415-919-8982 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Adrian Driscoll, Esq. 415-788-1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant CINDI PARDINI.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces there is no Opposition filed and Plaintiff did not contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues and requests leave to amend since he wishes to retain Counsel. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CINDI PARDINI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF CHRSITOPHER SILVA.

Sustained with leave to amend. No opposition filed.

By order dated March 21, 2019, the Court sustained Defendant's demurrer to the complaint with 35 days' leave to amend, and took Defendant's motion to strike the complaint off calendar as moot. The Court granted Plaintiff leave to plead a claim for slander and/or libel that (1) occurred within one year of the filing of the complaint on September 28, 2018, and (2) does not involve privileged conduct such as a police report or statements made in a judicial proceeding. The Court also directed Plaintiff to remove the allegations regarding time-barred or privileged conduct. On April 11, 2019, Plaintiff filed a 6-page [first amended] "complaint for damages" that fails to comply with the Court's prior order and largely suffers from the same defects as his original complaint. Although Plaintiff reportedly called Defendant's counsel on May 21 indicating his intent to provide an "amended filing," he has not opposed the motion.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Adrian G. Driscoll, esq. (415-788-1900) of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued, counsel presents proposed order. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CINDI PARDINI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT of Plaintiff Christopher Silva.

Defendant Pardini's demurrer is sustained with 20 days' leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead a claim for slander and/or libel that (1) occurred within a year of the filing of the instant complaint on September 28, 2018 (see Code Civ. Proc. 340(c)); and (2) does not involve privileged conduct such as a police report or statements made in a judicial proceeding. (See Civ. Code 47; Hagberg v. California Federal Bank (2004) 32 Cal.4th 39.) Plaintiff shall remove the allegations regarding time-barred or privileged conduct.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
